what_it_does: This file is an operational status report that inventories the OpenWebUI installations on Workstation001, documents their runtime state, configuration, backend connectivity, and provides startup/reconfiguration guidance.
observations: There are two OpenWebUI installs: a Docker install and a Python venv at /home/tim/openwebui-venv/
The Docker daemon is down/inactive and API calls return HTTP 500
A *:8080 socket is listening but dead, accepts connections without responding, and has no owning PID
The venv install is version 0.8.12 and contains a tiny, effectively unconfigured dataset (one user, no chats, no models)
The documented vLLM backends on ports 8000/8001 are dead
Ollama on 11434 and LiteLLM proxy on 4100 are live but not wired to OpenWebUI
Two ~988 MB Docker volume backups from 2026-05-28 exist
The report recommends starting the venv on an alternate port and wiring the live backends
standards: The orphaned 8080 listen socket with no owning PID and rising Recv-Q is the most anomalous finding
Docker is broken, blocking the documented primary install
The live backends (Ollama, LiteLLM) do not match the documented vLLM pair
The overall state is effectively a clean slate despite prior installation
conventions: Uses a markdown report structure with TL;DR, numbered sections, tables, and code blocks
Cites bash discovery evidence (ports, processes, sqlite, /proc)
References canonical foundation docs and backups as supporting evidence
Includes a premise from the lead that no precious data exists
concerns: The dead orphaned 8080 socket will block a fresh OpenWebUI bind on the default port
Docker daemon is non-functional under WSL2 but the socket file still exists
The documented Docker install references dead vLLM backends, creating stale operational guidance
Version mismatch between Docker image target (0.9.5) and venv (0.8.12)
notes: A forensic install-and-runtime report showing OpenWebUI is not running, Docker is broken, and the only live model surfaces are Ollama and LiteLLM rather than the documented vLLM pair.
questions: What caused the Docker daemon to fail and why does the socket still exist?
What process originally created the orphaned 8080 socket?
Where is the LiteLLM proxy API key stored?
Is the 0.9.5 Docker image actually present locally?
Why do the canonical docs still reference dead vLLM ports 8000/8001?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title is 'OpenWebUI — Install & Status Report' and the file delivers exactly that.
apparent_themes: Substrate drift between documentation and runtime
intention_signals: It appears intended to give the lead an accurate, evidence-based baseline for deciding how to restart or reconfigure OpenWebUI, correcting assumptions from stale docs.
novelty: The detailed characterization of a dead, ownerless 8080 socket with a climbing backlog is unusual; the report also explicitly invalidates the documented canonical install.
contribution: A neutral ground-truth inventory of install locations, versions, runtime state, backend liveness, port/socket blockage, and actionable startup guidance, plus explicit flags about stale documentation and data discardability.
summary_for_embedding: Status report documenting two OpenWebUI installations on Workstation001 (Docker and Python venv), neither serving, a dead orphaned 8080 socket, unconfigured venv data, a broken Docker daemon, and live Ollama/LiteLLM backends replacing dead documented vLLM targets.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a clean, accurate baseline for restarting or reconfiguring OpenWebUI by reconciling installed artifacts, runtime reality, and stale documentation.
